steps.title: Write in standard form and recognize the quadratic formula structure
steps.content:
Rewrite $7x^2-9x+c=5$ as $7x^2-9x+(c-5)=0$. By the quadratic formula, both solutions take the form:
$$x = \frac{9 \pm \sqrt{81 - 28(c-5)}}{14} = \frac{9 \pm \sqrt{221 - 28c}}{14}$$

steps.title: Match the given solution to extract the discriminant
steps.content:
The given solution is $x = \frac{9-\sqrt{725}}{14}$, which matches our formula with the minus sign and discriminant 725. Therefore:
$$221 - 28c = 725$$
$$-28c = 504$$
$$c = -18$$
